name: Puig d'en Cama Loop from Reus
name_original: Montañas de Prades – circular desde Reus por Puig d'en Cama
description: This difficult 21.3-mile mountain bike route in the Prades Mountains offers varied terrain, technical sections, and views from Puig d'en Cam
region_intro: Mountain biking around Reus offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged Prades Mountains to tranquil reservoirs and scenic countryside. The region features varied terrain, including technical sections, significant elevation changes, and singletrack. Riders can explore routes through forests, agricultural lands, and areas with natural obstacles like rock gardens. Best mountain bike trails in Reus The most popular mountain bike trail is Puig d'en Cama Loop from Reus, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route in the Prades Mountains offers impressive views and substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Riudecanyes Reservoir – Montbrió del Camp Town Hall loop from Estació de Misericòrdia, a moderate 27.3 miles (44.0 km) path. This route incorporates picturesque views of the Riudecanyes Reservoir and varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Montbrió del Camp Town Hall – View of Mont-roig del Camp loop from Reus, a 26.5 miles (42.7 km) trail leading through Mont-roig del Camp, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Mountain biking around Reus is defined by the challenging Prades Mountains, the scenic Riudecanyes Reservoir, and surrounding forests and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to demanding technical trails. The routes in Reus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Reus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Reus?
A: There are over 120 mountain bike trails around Reus, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored and shared many routes in the region.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for mountain biking in Reus?
A: The trails around Reus cater to all skill levels. You'll find approximately 20 easy routes, 80 moderate routes, and 28 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced riders can find suitable challenges.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking near Reus?
A: Mountain biking near Reus offers diverse landscapes. You can explore the rugged terrain of the Prades Mountains, enjoy picturesque views around the Riudecanyes Reservoir, or ride through scenic forests and agricultural lands, including olive oil production villages like Riudoms and Vinyols i els Arcs. Areas like Mont-roig del Camp also provide beautiful scenery between the mountains and the sea.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Reus?
A: Yes, Reus offers several easy, shorter rides suitable for families and beginners. These routes often traverse gentler agricultural landscapes and forests, providing a pleasant and accessible experience. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' to find the most suitable options.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Reus?
A: Many mountain bike routes around Reus are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32061488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir – Montbrió del Camp Town Hall loop from Estació de Misericòrdia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path.

Q: What are some interesting places or landmarks to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/597960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama (717 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenary Pine of l'Aleixar&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4392860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montbrió del Camp Town Hall&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic Riudecanyes Reservoir, which features an aqueduct near Castell d'Escornalbou.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Reus?
A: The mountain bike trails around Reus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain sections to scenic reservoir views, and the network's suitability for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for technical mountain biking near Reus?
A: For more technical mountain biking, the Prades Mountains provide a rugged and challenging backdrop with varied terrain, technical sections, and significant elevation changes. The Picarany area near Reus is also known for its diverse trails, including downhill sections, rock gardens, and singletrack with features like drops and jumps.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Reus?
A: Reus generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. The Mediterranean climate means mild winters, making it possible to ride even in colder months. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures, while summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred during that season.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a challenge, routes in the Prades Mountains offer substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945443851/puig-d-en-cama-loop-from-reus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama Loop from Reus&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 930 meters of elevation gain, providing impressive views and a demanding workout.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass through villages or offer opportunities for refreshments?
A: Many routes around Reus traverse agricultural landscapes and pass through charming villages known for olive oil production, such as Riudoms and Vinyols i els Arcs. While specific cafes or pubs on trails are not detailed, these villages often provide opportunities for refreshments and a taste of local life.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, there are several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23984206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenary Pine of l'Aleixar – Coll de la Batalla loop from Reus&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering over 53 km with significant elevation, offering a substantial day out on the trails.
tips: Baroque style church located in the town of Aleixar, in Baix Camp. In the Plaza de la Vila, where the church is located, there is a source of drinking water.
Spectacular views from the mountain located at 717 while altitude, in the municipality of La Selva del Camp, in the region of Baix Camp. At the top we find a geodesic vertex.
Small hermitage on a hill, 500 meters away, in the Selva del Camp. Both the façade and the interior of the hermitage are predominantly white.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike the Montañas de Prades – circular desde Reus por Puig d'en Cama route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route typically takes around 3 hours and 25 minutes to complete. However, given its difficult rating and technical sections, allow for extra time depending on your skill level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It requires very good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills due to its technical sections, loose ground, and significant climbs. Riders should be prepared for varied surfaces and potentially needing to push their bike in some parts.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly diverse and challenging. You'll encounter approximately 22.8 km of loose ground, 6.31 km of singletrack, 5.37 km of paved road, and 1.64 km of side road. Expect rocky sections, and be aware that pushing your bike might be necessary for about 312 meters after 14.3 km into the route.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Reus, you can typically begin and end your ride in Reus. Specific parking information for the trailhead isn't provided, but Reus offers various parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular and diverse scenery. The route traverses ancient forests, offers rocky landscapes, and provides fast trails with breathtaking views. From Puig d'en Cama (717 m), you'll get good views over the plain with the majestic Prades mountains behind you. The region is rich in flora, including pines, holm oaks, and scented plants like lavender and thyme.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several points of interest. You'll encounter the Church of Sant Martí and Public Fountain of Aleixar, reach the summit of Puig d'en Cama (717 m) which offers excellent views, and pass the Hermitage of Sant Pere del Puig.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Montañas de Prades region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking. Spring brings wildflowers and pleasant temperatures, while autumn offers vibrant foliage. Summer can be hot, and winter might present colder conditions at higher elevations. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Montañas de Prades region is generally welcoming to dogs, specific regulations for this particular mountain bike route are not detailed. Given the technical nature and potential for varied terrain, it might be challenging for some dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit for the distance and terrain, and always keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and for their safety.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking in the Montañas de Prades?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Montañas de Prades. However, always respect local regulations, private property, and any signage you encounter on the trail.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. Notable intersections include parts of Per les pedreres, Ruta del Carrasclet, E01, Pel camí de Prades, Camí de Sant Pere al Puig d'en Cama, Camí dels Masos, and la Riera de Salvià o del Salt, among others.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, pack essentials like plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers. A helmet is mandatory, and consider knee/elbow pads for the technical sections. A GPS device or the komoot app with the downloaded route is highly recommended for navigation.
